title: galium commune subsp. umbellatum
intro: Galium commune subsp. umbellatum, also known as Wood Sorrel or Umbellate Bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Wood Sorrel include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Wood Sorrel is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
